Mean Reversion Trading Strategies in Python

Mean reversion is a core pillar of quantitative trading. The idea: prices that stretch far from their recent mean tend to snap back, all else equal.

In this article we cover three flavors: simple z-score fades on stock indices, funding-rate mean reversion on perpetual futures, and pairs trading on cointegrated cryptos.

Statistical Foundations

The Augmented Dickey-Fuller test is the canonical check for stationarity. If the p-value is low, the series is mean-reverting. For crypto funding rates the series is almost always stationary by design.

Implementation

We use pandas for rolling statistics, numpy for vectorized operations, and a simple walk-forward backtest to avoid look-ahead bias.

Pseudocode: compute 20-day rolling mean and std, generate signal when z-score crosses threshold, hold until z-score reverts to 0.
